source.multipart_basis: Sample prompt explicitly delivers 'a CAD assembly of one 6 m penstock pipe segment + one saddle support (concrete block + steel cradle + rubber liner)' as four distinct bodies; load path is pipe -> liner -> cradle -> concrete block.

full_prompt: Design one representative mild-steel penstock pipe segment + concrete saddle support for a 10 kW village-scale micro-hydropower scheme per Practical Action's Micro-hydro Design Manual and Technical Brief series, delivered AS A MULTI-PART ASSEMBLY of four distinct bodies: (1) 6 m mild-steel penstock pipe segment, (2) mass-concrete saddle block, (3) curved steel saddle cradle, (4) rubber liner between pipe and cradle. The scheme has a gross head of 60 m, design flow 0.05 m3/s, and a penstock diameter of 200 mm OD mild-steel pipe (spiral-welded, wall thickness 4 mm, grade ASTM A53 Gr B, yield 240 MPa) with a single inline saddle support. Design internal pressure is the static head 60 m = 588 kPa plus a 30 percent surge/water-hammer allowance per Practical Action guidance giving 765 kPa design pressure. The penstock segment under evaluation is 6.0 m long between two saddle supports, with the governing saddle being a mass-concrete block 600 mm x 600 mm x 500 mm embedded in the slope, bearing the pipe with a curved steel cradle lined with rubber. The saddle must resist: (a) vertical dead + water weight of the 6 m pipe segment, approximately m_pipe = rho_steel * V_steel + rho_water * V_water = 7850 * pi * (0.2^2-0.192^2)/4 * 6 + 1000 * pi * 0.192^2/4 * 6 = 116 + 174 = 290 kg, giving 2850 N per saddle, (b) horizontal friction force along pipe axis from thermal expansion = mu_friction * normal = 0.3 * 2850 = 855 N, (c) at bends the thrust from internal pressure (this saddle is nominally at a straight run; bend thrust handled by separate thrust block out of scope). For ropeway ancillary: a single 6x19 galvanized steel carrier cable with safety factor >= 5 per Practical Action ropeway brief is referenced but kept as an out-of-scope secondary artifact. Joinery: steel cradle is cast-in / anchored into the concrete block via four M16 J-bolts embedded 300 mm; rubber liner is bonded to cradle with contact adhesive; pipe rests in cradle with no axial clamp (free axial sliding under thermal expansion, governed by friction). Deliverable is a multi-part CAD assembly suitable for linear static structural FEA under (a) 765 kPa internal pressure (hoop + longitudinal), (b) vertical saddle reaction 2850 N with realistic bearing footprint, (c) axial friction 855 N, with the assembled-only FEM gate reporting both pipe and saddle metrics. Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, radiation analyses NOT required. Water hammer is handled as the static 30 percent surge factor; detailed transient fluid simulation is optional.
short_prompt:
Design a Practical Action micro-hydro penstock pipe + saddle + anchor-block assembly. Pipe 6 m long x 200 mm OD x 4 mm wall, ASTM A53 Gr B steel (yield 240 MPa); hydraulic head 60 m (static pressure 765 kPa).

Load cases: LC1 static 765 kPa internal pressure; LC2 thrust 2850 N at bend; LC3 dead + live saddle reaction 855 N.

Pass/fail: hoop stress within yield with SF 2.0; saddle support SF >= 1.5 against sliding; anchor block mass resists LC2 thrust with SF; FEM at assembled level; weld and flange DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Optional 1D method-of-characteristics transient hydraulic simulation can be run to refine the 30% surge factor; not a CAE FEA.

prompt.title: Micro-hydro penstock + saddle for 10 kW village scheme (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 6 m segment of 200 mm OD mild-steel penstock + concrete saddle block + steel cradle + rubber liner as a four-part assembly for 60 m head and 765 kPa design pressure per Practical Action.

parts.role: primary pressure-containing pipe spanning between saddles
parts.notes: Single 6 m ASTM A53 Gr B pipe; carries 765 kPa internal design pressure.

parts.role: mass-concrete foundation block transferring saddle reaction to soil
parts.notes: One mass-concrete block per saddle; footing area 0.36 m2 to limit bearing pressure.

parts.role: curved steel cradle accepting the pipe OD and anchoring to the concrete block

geometric_constraints.anchor_bolts: 4 x M16 J-bolts at 300 mm embedment

parts.notes: 6 mm bent plate or half-sleeve welded to anchor straps.

parts.role: rubber bearing liner between pipe OD and steel cradle
parts.notes: 60 shore A rubber, bonded to cradle to spread pipe bearing and accommodate thermal expansion.

penstock.grade: ASTM A53 Gr B mild steel

objective.primary: pass all criteria on the assembled model

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: gross head + 30% surge per Practical Action
typical soil allowable bearing for compacted fill / weathered rock
Practical Action ropeway brief (cross-referenced, not analyzed in this sample)
pass_fail_criteria.note: Documented for traceability; ropeway is a separate artifact.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: assembled_only FEM gate runs LC1/LC2/LC3 on the full pipe+liner+cradle+block assembly; R1-R4 must pass together on the assembled geometry

analysis_types: linear static internal pressure + saddle reaction + friction

interfaces.detail: pipe OD sliding on rubber liner with friction coefficient 0.3; no axial clamp
interfaces.load_path: vertical pipe dead+water weight distributed into liner arc

interfaces.detail: rubber liner bonded to steel cradle with contact adhesive
interfaces.load_path: bearing pressure from pipe transferred to cradle plate

interfaces.detail: steel cradle anchored to concrete block via 4 x M16 J-bolts cast 300 mm deep
interfaces.load_path: vertical reaction and axial friction transferred into concrete block

fem_check_policy.rationale: Saddle bearing pressure, pipe hoop stress, and sliding stability are coupled across pipe+liner+cradle+block; a per-part FEM check would lose the load transfer chain that governs R3/R4.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal expansion captured as friction LC3 only.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Surge handled as 30% static; transient optional.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: External-pressure buckling not governing at 60 m head; long-span pipe buckling handled by saddle spacing.

uncertainty_flags.note: Count minima speculated from Practical Action saddle drawing (pipe+liner+cradle+block); cradle may be field-welded as two half-shells in some builds.

eval_coverage: pressure-pipe structural FEA with surge factor on assembled pipe+saddle
saddle bearing + sliding stability through cradle+block interface
